Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Raymond Chen's QC spot check findings using search_emails (query: "Raymond" or "QC" or "spot check" or "condition cleared" or similar). Expected discovery: Raymond's email to Grace detailing three files where conditions were marked cleared in LOS without supporting docs in the file. The three affected loans are LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224. Raymond's email states one file has already closed, and his Slack message identifies LN-2026-00603 as the closed file. The LOS audit trail shows Tyler Washington clearing the conditions on all three. Raymond also notes Tyler had flagged workload strain in Slack and asked Elena whether people clear conditions before uploading docs.
OE 2: Search emails for Tyler Washington's explanation using search_emails (query: "Tyler Washington" or "Context before 2:30" or similar). Expected discovery: Tyler's pre-meeting email to Grace acknowledging he cleared conditions before docs were in the file. His explanation cites workload overload, multiple lock files hitting simultaneously, and that on LN-2026-00603 specifically, Carlos was pushing hard to keep it moving before the lock expired. Tyler says he made a bad call trying to keep the file from blowing up and is not dodging responsibility.
OE 3: Search emails for Grace's post-meeting follow-up using search_emails (query: "Denise" + "QC" or "Tyler" + "conditions" or similar). Expected discovery: Grace's email to Denise and Raymond summarizing the Tyler meeting outcome, Tyler admitted clearing conditions before docs were uploaded, explained it as workload/lock pressure, and said he understood it as something people sometimes fixed after the fact. Grace asks Denise for repurchase/compliance risk assessment on the closed file and whether broader corrective action is needed.
OE 4: Search emails for any response from Denise Holloway about compliance exposure on the QC files using search_emails (query: "Denise" + "exposure" or "repurchase" or "investor risk" or similar). Expected discovery: Grace's emails TO Denise exist, but no reply from Denise specifically addressing the QC compliance question is found, confirming the compliance question is still unresolved and needs follow-up.
OE 5: Search Slack for Tyler Washington's workload messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "underwater" or "buried" or "Tyler" or "LN-2026-00603" or similar). Expected discovery: Tyler's message in #loan-processing (C002): "Anyone else underwater today? I'm buried in conds + closing stuff and the lock files are all hitting at once. LN-2026-00603 is still hanging over me and I have 2 others needing final touches before EOD." Also Raymond's Slack message: "Grace, just emailed you the QC summary. 3 Tyler-cleared files w missing support docs, incl LN-2026-00603 which already closed." Also Grace's Slack message scheduling the QC follow-up for 9:30.
OE 6: Look up each QC-affected loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00603") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00603"), and repeat for LN-2026-00211 and LN-2026-00224. Expected discovery: LN-2026-00603, $260,000 conventional purchase, status processing, assigned LO Natasha Okafor, rate lock expiration 2026-04-26. LN-2026-00211, $217,000 FHA purchase, status withdrawn, assigned LO Brian Mitchell (inactive), rate lock expiration 2026-04-09. LN-2026-00224, $184,000 USDA purchase, status closed, assigned LO Marcus Webb, closing date 2026-03-10. Note: the email/Slack narrative identifies LN-2026-00603 as the closed file delivered to the investor, but the LOS shows LN-2026-00603 as processing and LN-2026-00224 as the actually closed loan, this cross-service discrepancy is part of the investigation the agent must reconcile.
OE 7: Search CRM engagement notes for QC-related entries using crm_list_engagements (filtered by relevant contact_ids or company_ids, or listing all engagements). Expected discovery: Multiple CRM engagement notes documenting the QC incident: "QC spot check found 3 cleared conds with no PDF support in file. One closed loan may have post-close exposure." and "QC pulled 3 exception files. LN-2026-00603 already closed with cleared conds lacking file support; Tyler audit trail on all 3." and "Post-meeting w Tyler. Admitted conds were cleared before docs uploaded; said workload/lock pressure and informal practice drove it. Decision pending: conduct issue vs training gap vs staffing/process failure."
OE 8: Search emails for Victor Osei's production report on Carlos Rivera using search_emails (query: "Victor" or "Carlos Rivera production" or "funded commissions" or similar). Expected discovery: Victor's email to Grace with subject "Carlos Rivera production snapshot": "Carlos Rivera has generated $0 in funded commissions over the last 60 days. I also don't see any funded loans in that period that would create trailing payout this cycle."
OE 9: Search emails for Grace's performance assessment and internal notes on Carlos using search_emails (query: "Rivera review" or "Carlos" + "performance" or "PIP" or similar). Expected discovery: Grace's self-addressed review notes listing the full pattern: zero funded commissions over 60 days, two preventable lock expirations, repeated processor friction around incomplete files and cleanup lag, Jordan getting direct partner requests not to place new buyers with Carlos, active pipeline stalled. Also Grace's formal PIP recommendation email to Robert detailing the same pattern and recommending reassignment of the most vulnerable active files.
OE 10: Search Slack for internal discussion about Carlos Rivera's pipeline using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Carlos" + "pipeline" or "PIP" or "funded" or similar). Expected discovery: Grace's Slack messages: "Pulling Carlos together now. Between the 2 lock misses, partner noise, and no funded deals in 60 days, this is feeling PIP-level" and "Robert, I think we're past coaching-only on Carlos. I'm leaning PIP based on file quality, the 2 lock misses, partner complaints, and 60 days with no funded comp" and "Sent Carlos the formal notice."
OE 11: Search LOS for Carlos Rivera's active loan pipeline using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Carlos Rivera" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: Carlos's staff ID discovered via mortgage_los_list_staff). Expected discovery: Carlos's active loans including LN-2026-00009 ($393,000 VA purchase, status conditional_approval, closing date 2026-04-12, rate lock expiration 2026-04-15) and other active files. Check each loan's status, rate lock expirations, and closing dates to identify approaching or past-due deadlines.
OE 12: Search CRM deals for Carlos Rivera's loans using crm_search_deals (dealname: loan number or borrower name from Carlos's pipeline, e.g. "LN-2026-00009" or similar) or crm_show_data to browse deals. Expected discovery: CRM deals for Carlos's loans exist primarily for older pipeline files (2024-2025 vintage), with descriptions noting "LO: Carlos Rivera." Deal stages and amounts provide context on Carlos's historical pipeline activity. Current 2026 pipeline files may not have corresponding CRM deal records.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for financial data related to Carlos Rivera's commission impact using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: filtering by loan numbers from Carlos's pipeline, e.g. "LN-2026-00009", or by borrower names associated with his loans) or quickbooks_show_data to browse invoice records. Expected discovery: Broker compensation invoices are keyed to borrower names and loan numbers, not LO names. Older Carlos loans (2024-2025) have matching commission invoices, but no recent commission invoices exist for the last 60-day window, confirming Victor's production snapshot of $0 funded commissions.
OE 14: Look up Denise Holloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise Holloway" or similar). Expected result: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 15: Look up Elena Marchetti's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Marchetti" or similar). Expected result: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 16: Look up Robert Calloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert Calloway" or similar). Expected result: r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 17: Add activity note to QC-affected loan LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: discovered internal loan ID for LN-2026-00603, action: "note" or similar, detail: "Under ops and compliance review per QC spot check findings. Conditions were cleared without supporting documentation in file. No status changes authorized until further notice." or similar, author: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com" or similar).
OE 18: Add activity note to QC-affected loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: discovered internal loan ID for LN-2026-00211, action: "note" or similar, detail: same ops/compliance review hold note, author: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com" or similar).
OE 19: Add activity note to QC-affected loan LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: discovered internal loan ID for LN-2026-00224, action: "note" or similar, detail: same ops/compliance review hold note, author: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com" or similar).
OE 20: Send email to Denise Holloway about compliance exposure on the QC files using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: related to compliance exposure or QC follow-up on closed loan, content: covering the QC findings, conditions cleared without supporting docs on three files, with LN-2026-00224 confirmed closed per LOS (closing date 2026-03-10) representing the post-close compliance exposure, while the email/Slack narrative references LN-2026-00603 as the closed file, requesting Denise's assessment of investor and regulatory risk on the closed file, and noting this is a follow-up to the earlier request that may not have been answered).
OE 21: Send email to Elena Marchetti about re-verifying the non-closed QC files using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: related to QC review / files needing re-verification, content: identifying the affected files from Raymond's findings that are not yet closed and delivered, per the email narrative LN-2026-00211 and LN-2026-00603 are the non-closed files that need re-verification (LN-2026-00224 is closed per LOS with closing date 2026-03-10), and asking Elena to pull them for proper condition re-verification before anything else moves on them).
OE 22: Send email to Robert Calloway with consolidated operational brief using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: related to operational review or QC + Carlos performance, content: summarizing both the QC findings, three files affected, Tyler Washington as the processor who cleared conditions without supporting docs, LN-2026-00224 confirmed closed per LOS with compliance exposure, remaining files flagged for re-verification, and Carlos Rivera's pipeline status: zero funded commissions in 60+ days, two lock expirations, processor complaints about incomplete files, partner pullback, and current active file status with deadlines).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
